title: Distinguish missing from corrupt phase
goal: readPhaseFile distinguishes ENOENT (returns null) from malformed JSON or read errors (throws a clear error naming the path and reason), so loadPhase, assembleState and verify/adversarial report the real problem instead of a misleading 'no phase', and corrupt phase detail is never silently overwritten on the next save.

steps.title: Add CorruptPhaseError type
steps.detail: New error class in src/state/store.ts carrying the file path and original cause; readable message like 'Corrupt phase file <path>: <reason>'. Exported so tests/CLI can react to it.

steps.title: readPhaseFile distinguishes ENOENT from corrupt
steps.detail: In readPhaseFile (store.ts:178-185): err.code === 'ENOENT' returns null (missing); any other read error or JSON.parse failure throws CorruptPhaseError with path and cause. No blanket catch returning null.

steps.title: Propagate through loaders, no silent fallback
steps.detail: Ensure the error bubbles through loadPhase, assembleState (the ?? {id,title,status} fallback stays only for null/missing, not for throw), loadFullState and loadPrev, so corrupt detail never degrades to header and a later save never overwrites it. Audit loadPhase callers (verify/adversarial/security/context/range) so no try/catch swallows it or turns it back into a misleading 'no phase' — that message may only fire for a genuinely missing phase.

steps.title: Tests: corrupt vs missing
steps.detail: In src/state/store.test.ts: (a) corrupt phase-<id>.json (e.g. git conflict markers) makes loadPhase and loadFullState throw CorruptPhaseError with the right path; (b) missing file still returns null; (c) 167-1 regression: a degraded state is never written back after a failed load (load throws before mutate/save).

steps.title: Verify build, typecheck and tests pass
steps.detail: npm run build, typecheck and the full test suite green.

autoCommit.subject: Phase 176: Distinguish missing from corrupt phase
